metadata.notes: Eval 3 required one SKILL.md iteration before passing: iteration 1 produced major/high-confidence severity for the type mislabel; the proportionality guidance was sharpened to explicitly anchor chore-vs-story mismatches at minor/medium-confidence. All other evals passed on iteration 1.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
At least one finding has severity 'major' or 'critical'
Finding is located in Summary or Requirements
expectations.evidence: Critical finding in Summary: 'Epic bundles four unrelated product capabilities with no unifying theme'.

expectations.text: Finding body identifies that the epic bundles unrelated themes and recommends splitting into separate epics
expectations.evidence: Body names all four themes (user profile rework, billing migration, push notifications, audit logs) and explicitly suggests splitting into four separate epics.

expectations.text: No finding flags missing sections or unmeasurable criteria
expectations.evidence: All three findings are scope-boundary observations (bundling, multi-team ownership, done-condition coupling). No completeness or testability bleed.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
At least one finding has severity 'major' or 'critical', located in Requirements or Summary
expectations.evidence: 1 critical in Summary + 1 major in Requirements.

expectations.text: Finding notes two independent units of work: CSV export feature and large framework migration (3-4 weeks)
expectations.evidence: Critical finding explicitly names CSV export (Requirements 1-2) and grid migration (Requirements 3-4) as orthogonal workstreams with independent value.

expectations.text: Finding recommends splitting into separate tickets
expectations.evidence: Suggestion: 'Split into two tickets — one story for the CSV export feature and a separate story or epic for the grid framework migration.'

expectations.text: No finding about ambiguous language or missing acceptance criteria as scope issues
expectations.evidence: Both findings are scope observations (bundling and sizing). No clarity or testability bleed.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
No finding at 'critical' or 'major' severity
At least one finding at 'minor' or 'suggestion' severity
Finding is located in Summary or Frontmatter: type
Finding confidence is 'medium'
No finding about missing required sections
expectations.evidence: Only 1 finding, a proportional type-mislabel observation. No completeness bleed.

notes: Iteration 1 produced major/high-confidence severity — the proportionality guidance was too weak. After sharpening the 'Be proportional' bullet in Important Guidelines to explicitly anchor chore-vs-story mismatches at minor/medium-confidence, the eval passed on iteration 2.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
At least one finding at 'major' or 'critical', located in Acceptance Criteria or Summary
expectations.evidence: 1 critical in Summary: 'Spike research question is entirely unbounded'.

expectations.text: Finding notes the research question is too broad and unbounded (not merely that exit criteria are unmeasurable)
expectations.evidence: Critical finding addresses the research question itself ('explore and understand the company's microservices architecture') as open-ended with no decision point — distinct from measurability of criteria.

expectations.text: No finding flags exit criteria wording as unmeasurable without first flagging the underlying question as too broad
expectations.evidence: All three findings target the scope/bounding problem (unbounded question, undefined framing, unbounded survey requirements). No testability bleed.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
At least one finding at 'minor' or higher severity
Finding addresses over-decomposition — twelve micro-tasks that could be one story, not an epic with twelve children
expectations.evidence: Major: 'Epic type is inappropriate for trivially small, atomic refactor' — notes twelve renames deliverable in one commit. Minor: child stories are not independently releasable.

expectations.text: Finding is in Frontmatter: type, Requirements, or Stories
expectations.evidence: Major finding in Frontmatter: type; minor finding in Stories.

expectations.text: No finding about missing context or unclear acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: Both findings are structural scope observations. No completeness or clarity bleed.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
At least one finding at 'major' or 'critical', located in Requirements or Summary
expectations.evidence: 1 critical in Requirements + 1 major in Summary.

expectations.text: Finding notes ticket spans three distinct service boundaries (inventory, billing, notification) with no owning team or orchestration decision
expectations.evidence: Critical finding names all three teams (inventory, payments, platform) and notes no owning team or orchestration mechanism.

expectations.text: Finding recommends splitting into per-service tickets plus coordination
expectations.evidence: Suggestion: 'decompose into a thin orchestrator story owned by one team plus per-service stories owned by each respective team, linked under an epic.'

expectations.text: No finding about missing ordering of operations as a dependency concern
expectations.evidence: Both findings are scope observations (service boundaries, sizing). No dependency-lens bleed about ordering.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
No finding at 'critical' or 'major' severity
Strengths list notes coherent single-capability scope
expectations.evidence: Strengths include: 'All three substantive sections describe exactly the same scope' and 'strictly front-end in nature... making team ownership and service boundaries unambiguous'.

expectations.text: No finding alleging the ticket mixes multiple concerns
No finding alleging unbounded exploration, over/under-decomposition, or service-boundary spanning
Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
Zero findings at 'critical' or 'major' severity
At most one minor/suggestion finding total
Strengths list notes bounded scope (three specific libraries, three-day time-box) and concrete exit criteria
expectations.evidence: Strengths include: 'three named libraries... four named criteria'; 'time-box (3 working days) is explicit'; 'produces a concrete, named deliverable'.

expectations.text: No finding about sizing, bundling, decomposition, or unbounded exploration
Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='scope'
Zero findings at 'critical' or 'major' severity
At most one minor/suggestion finding total
Strengths list notes coherent epic scope and well-articulated decomposition (three child stories serving one capability)
expectations.evidence: Strengths include: 'epic names exactly one user-visible capability'; 'three child stories are genuinely cohesive — each addresses a different layer'; 'neither over-decomposed nor under-decomposed'.

expectations.text: No finding mentioning bundling, over-decomposition, or under-decomposition

notes: All 9 evals pass at iteration 2. Iteration 1 had 8/9 passing — eval 3 (undersized-standalone-story) produced major/high-confidence for a type-mislabel (chore as story), whereas the eval expects minor/medium-confidence. The proportionality guidance in Important Guidelines was sharpened to explicitly anchor chore-vs-story mismatches as minor/suggestion at medium confidence, since what is 'chore-like' depends on team norms the reviewer cannot observe.
Positive fixtures (evals 7-9) all produced 0 findings with strong strengths notes — no false positives observed on clean tickets.

Token counts are notably lower than the completeness-lens benchmark (~7700 vs ~14500) because the scope lens reviewer reads fewer files (lens SKILL.md + output format + ticket, no multiple tools). Timing is similarly faster (12-30s vs 27-41s).

Single-run evaluation cannot surface non-deterministic failure modes. Multi-run evaluation (3 runs per eval) would be needed to detect edge-case drift, especially on the positive fixtures where false positives are the primary risk.
